headline: Mobia Medical poised for commercial expansion, BofA says in initial coverage
summary: Mobia Medical (NASDAQ:MOBI) was initiated with a ‘Buy’ rating and a $16 price target by Bank of America, which cited the company's growth potential in stroke recovery treatment and a large addressable market for its Vivistim system. Mobia's Vivistim therapy combines rehabilitation exercises...

headline: FedEx Freight initiated at ‘Buy’ by Bank of America on margin expansion potential
summary: FedEx Freight (NYSE:FDXF) shares traded around $152 on Tuesday afternoon after Bank of America initiated coverage of the newly independent less-than-truckload (LTL) carrier with a ‘Buy’ rating and a $185 price target. The initiation comes one day after FedEx Freight was spun off from FedEx...

headline: Wall Street Analysts Turn Skeptical After Two-Month Stocks Rally
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US stocks may be vaulting from one record to the next, but Wall Street analysts covering them are in no rush to keep up. headline: European Media, Telecom Capital Market Activity Rises To $3.39B In April
summary: Publicly traded media and telecommunications companies in Europe raised $3.39 billion through capital offerings in April, surpassing the previous month and a year earlier. Read more here.

headline: How A Flat Price Target Is Shaping The Story For Bank Of America (BAC)
summary: Bank of America’s latest analyst update comes with no change to the stock’s price target, leaving investors with the same reference point as before. Even without a fresh price target or new commentary to dissect, there are still useful signals in what analysts are not changing. Read on to see how to interpret a flat price target and how to keep tracking the evolving narrative around Bank of America. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story.
